1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to Fizeau are recorded here.
4	Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.
5	
6	## [Unreleased]
7	
8	### Added
9	
10	- `openaicompat.Config.ConnectTimeout` (defaults to 5 s via
11	  `openaicompat.DefaultConnectTimeout`): explicit bound on the TCP connect
12	  phase for OpenAI-compatible provider HTTP traffic. The default
13	  `http.Transport` is replaced with one whose `DialContext` uses a
14	  `net.Dialer{Timeout: ConnectTimeout}`, so an unreachable endpoint surfaces a
15	  dial-class failure in ≤5 s instead of waiting the kernel's full ~30 s SYN-
16	  retransmit window. Callers on high-latency networks (DDx, benchmarks) can
17	  raise the bound by setting `ConnectTimeout` on the config; zero or negative
18	  values fall back to the 5 s default. The request-streaming timeout is
19	  unchanged — large completions can still take minutes once connected.
20	
21	## [v0.14.0] — 2026-05-14
22	
23	### Added
24	
25	- `RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes` (`[]ExcludedRoute`): callers can now supply a
26	  list of `(Provider, Model, Endpoint)` combinations to exclude from routing.
27	  The router skips any candidate matching an entry and records it with the new
28	  `FilterReasonCallerExcluded` (`"caller_excluded"`) filter reason, so
29	  routing-quality observability can distinguish caller-driven re-routes from
30	  internal health signals. `Model` and `Endpoint` are optional — an empty value
31	  matches any. Propagated from the public `ResolveRoute` surface through to the
32	  internal routing engine.
33	
34	### Fixed
35	
36	- **Connect-time fast-fail**: dial-level TCP failures (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT,
37	  EHOSTUNREACH — detected as `*net.OpError{Op: "dial"}`) no longer run through
38	  the 4-attempt exponential retry curve. A dead local endpoint now fails in
39	  under 5 s instead of burning ~2 m 45 s of wall-clock per call. In-flight
40	  5xx / rate-limit errors retain the existing retry behavior.
41	
42	### Breaking — v0.11 Routing Surface
43	
44	- Routing intent is now policy-based. Public callers use `--policy`,
45	  `--min-power`, and `--max-power`; hard pins remain `--model`, `--provider`,
46	  and `--harness`.
47	- Removed routing flags and service fields for the old route-reference surface:
48	  `--profile`, `--model-ref`, `ModelRef`, `ListProfiles`, `ResolveProfile`,
49	  and `ProfileAliases`.
50	- `fiz policies` and `fiz harnesses` expose the supported v0.11 inspection
51	  surfaces. Route-status JSON uses `policy` and `power_policy`.
52	- The canonical policy names are `cheap`, `default`, `smart`, and
53	  `air-gapped`. The dropped policy/alias names are: `fast`, `code-fast`,
54	  `code-economy`, `code-smart`, `standard`, `local`, `offline`, `code-high`,
55	  and `code-medium`.
56	- Pay-per-token providers are default-deny for automatic routing unless
57	  `include_by_default` opts them in. Explicit pins can still select excluded
58	  providers, but policy requirements such as `air-gapped` / `no_remote` still
59	  win.
60	
61	## [v0.10.9] — 2026-05-06
62	
63	### Changed
64	
65	- Sticky utilization routing now records and replays the evidence used to
66	  choose a destination, including endpoint load and route-health state, so the
67	  public routing projections can explain why a sticky route won.
68	- In-process sticky route leases now back the routing behavior for local model
69	  variants, with provider utilization probes normalized across `llama-server`,
70	  `vllm`, `rapid-mlx`, and `omlx`.
71	- Provider and route-status surfaces now carry the sticky-utilization evidence
72	  needed by the service facade and session replay helpers.
73	
74	### Tests
75	
76	- Coverage was added or refreshed for sticky route selection, route-health
77	  utilization storage, provider utilization probes, and cassette-based
78	  utilization recordings for the local server providers.
79	
80	## [v0.10.8] — 2026-05-05
81	
82	### Changed
83	
84	- Root `package fizeau` is now the public service facade for the cleanup
85	  covered by ADR-008. Concrete execution dispatch, transcript/progress
86	  rendering, route-health feedback, provider quota state, burn-rate tracking,
87	  and routing-quality aggregation now live behind internal package boundaries.
88	- Fizeau owns the public transcript/progress semantics for native and
89	  subprocess harness paths, so downstream consumers can consume service events
90	  and projections without parsing harness-native streams or private session-log
91	  records.
92	- Pure no-op compaction remains silent at the public service boundary; stale
93	  downstream no-op compaction stall policy should migrate away from that signal.
94	
95	### Tests
96	
97	- Public facade smoke coverage now exercises constructor/service import,
98	  progress events, route feedback/status, usage-window validation,
99	  provider quota wrappers, burn-rate wrappers, and routing-quality metric
100	  structs from `package fizeau_test`.

9	# Feature Specification: FEAT-004 — Shared Model Catalog and Policy Routing
10	
11	**Feature ID**: FEAT-004
12	**Status**: Draft
13	**Priority**: P0 (provider sources/endpoints), P1 (catalog), P1 (routing)
14	**Owner**: Fizeau Team
15	
16	## Overview
17	
18	Fizeau routes requests by evaluating `route(client_inputs, fiz_models_snapshot)`.
19	Client inputs include policy/profile, pins, `no_remote`, metered opt-in, tools,
20	context, reasoning needs, and other explicit constraints. The `fiz models`
21	snapshot is the only source of routing facts: health, quota, model
22	availability, effective cost, `actual_cash_spend`, billing kind, context/tools/
23	reasoning support, locality, reliability, latency, utilization, and per-field
24	freshness.
25	
26	The snapshot is assembled from configured provider sources and harnesses,
27	discovered model IDs, catalog metadata, and runtime signals joined into one set
28	of provider/model facts. Fizeau has no required daemon. Its freshness contract
29	is synchronous refresh plus cross-process locks, single-flight coalescing, TTLs,
30	cooldowns, bounded concurrency, and atomic snapshot writes. A long-running DDx
31	server may call the same Fizeau refresh entrypoints to keep snapshot facts warm,
32	but route correctness must not depend on that process.
33	
34	The public v0.11 routing surface is:
35	
36	- `Policy`: one of `cheap`, `default`, `smart`, or `air-gapped`;
37	- `MinPower` and `MaxPower`: numeric soft power hints on the 1..10 catalog
38	  scale;
39	- hard pins: `Harness`, `Provider`, and exact `Model`.
40	
41	Deprecated route tables, model reference aliases, compatibility targets, and
42	surface policy projections are not public routing controls.
43	
44	This feature spec defines the required routing behavior and public contracts.
45	`SD-005` owns the implementation sequence, cache mechanics, candidate scoring
46	formula, and routing trace construction.
47	
48	## Problem Statement
49	
50	- Provider config should describe transport/auth, not route policy.
51	- The catalog should own model metadata, power, cost, deployment class,
52	  auto-routable status, and provider surface strings.
53	- Callers need a small, explainable routing vocabulary that avoids accidental
54	  paid spend but still allows exact pins and explicit escalation.
55	
56	## Terminology
57	
58	- **Policy**: a named bundle of power bounds, local allowance, and hard
59	  requirements.
60	- **Power**: catalog model-strength integer from 1 to 10. `0` means unknown or
61	  exact-pin-only.
62	- **Hard pin**: caller assertion on `Harness`, `Provider`, or exact `Model`.
63	- **Route candidate**: one `(harness, provider, endpoint, model)` option after
64	  live discovery and catalog join.
65	- **Default inclusion**: provider-level `include_by_default`, used only for
66	  unpinned automatic routing.
67	- **Metered opt-in**: operator permission for pay-per-token candidates to
68	  participate in unpinned automatic routing. Provider default inclusion is still
69	  required.
70	- **Effective cost**: normalized request-local scoring cost. Subscription
71	  candidates use PAYG-equivalent pricing for comparison even when dispatch does
72	  not create actual cash spend.
73	- **Actual cash spend**: whether dispatching the candidate creates incremental
74	  pay-per-token billing. This is separate from effective cost.
75	- **Unpinned request**: a request with no `Harness`, no `Provider`, and no exact
76	  `Model`. `Policy`, `MinPower`, `MaxPower`, `Reasoning`, capability flags, and
77	  token estimates do not make a request pinned.
78	- **Sticky affinity**: a score bonus for reusing a server instance for related
79	  requests when the candidate is still eligible.
80	
81	## Requirements
82	
83	### Catalog and Manifest
84	
85	1. The manifest schema is v5.
86	2. Catalog models carry concrete model ID, provider surface strings, power,
87	   auto-routable/exact-pin-only status, deployment class, cost, context,
88	   benchmark provenance, capabilities, and reasoning defaults.
89	3. Catalog policies carry `min_power`, `max_power`, `allow_local`, and
90	   `require[]`.
91	4. Catalog providers carry provider `type`, `include_by_default`, and explicit
92	   billing only when the hardcoded provider-system table cannot infer billing.
93	5. Removed v0.10 routing concepts (`target`, aliases as routing personas, and
94	   user-visible `surface_policy`) must not be presented as public routing API.
95	   Narrow compatibility structs may exist only to keep older internal catalog
96	   readers working while the primary v5 shape is used.
97	6. Ordinary execution uses the embedded or configured local manifest. It does
98	   not fetch manifest updates over the network.
99	
100	### Canonical Policies
101	
102	7. The canonical policy set is exactly:
103	
104	| Policy | MinPower | MaxPower | AllowLocal | Require | Intent |
105	|--------|----------|----------|------------|---------|--------|
106	| `cheap` | 5 | 5 | true | none | minimize effective cost; local/fixed candidates preferred |
107	| `default` | 7 | 8 | true | none | balanced default; local/fixed or healthy subscription can win |
108	| `smart` | 9 | 10 | false | none | quality-first; subscription/cloud-capable candidates preferred |
109	| `air-gapped` | 5 | 5 | true | `no_remote` | local-only execution; remote/account providers rejected |
110	
111	8. `ListPolicies` returns these canonical entries and manifest metadata. It
112	   does not list dropped compatibility names.
113	9. `allow_local=false` disallows local/fixed candidates for that policy unless
114	   the caller changes policy or requirements.
115	10. `require[]` currently supports `no_remote`. Unknown requirements fail
116	    validation instead of being ignored.
117	11. `no_remote` rejects remote or account-billed candidates even when the
118	    caller pins a provider or harness.
119	
120	### Assembled Routing Inventory
121	
122	12. `ResolveRoute`, `RouteStatus`, and `fiz models` use the same assembled
123	    snapshot as their routing inventory contract. The router must not maintain a
124	    second discovery view that can diverge from operator-visible model facts.
125	    `ResolveRoute` is the public `route(client_inputs, fiz_models_snapshot)`
126	    contract.
127	13. The assembled snapshot contains one identity per discovered
128	    `(provider, model_id)` pair, including harness-as-provider identities for
129	    subscription harnesses. Catalog-only models do not appear as available
130	    models unless a configured source actually serves them.
131	14. Live discovery wins over configured model hints. A configured default model
132	    is a fallback hint when discovery is unavailable, not a closed inventory.
133	15. Discovered model IDs may be matched to catalog metadata when the mapping is
134	    unambiguous. Unknown models remain inspectable and exact-pinnable, but are
135	    not eligible for unpinned automatic routing.
136	16. The route decision trace records selected, eligible, and rejected
137	    candidates with typed reasons. Consumers must use typed fields, not parse
138	    human-readable reason strings.
139	17. Test-only harnesses never leak into policy-based routing unless explicitly
140	    requested.
141	
142	### Snapshot Freshness
143	
144	18. `fiz models` is quick by default. It reads the current assembled snapshot,
145	    returns available stale data with freshness metadata, and does not block on
146	    slow discovery or runtime probes.
147	19. `fiz models` may request a best-effort background refresh for stale fields,
148	    but only through the same refresh coordinator, cross-process locks, and
149	    single-flight markers used by blocking refresh. A short-lived CLI process
150	    must not spawn independent probe storms or imply that background refresh is
151	    required for correctness.
152	20. `fiz models --refresh` blocks until routing-relevant stale fields have been
153	    refreshed or conclusively failed. `fiz models --refresh-all` blocks on every
154	    refreshable field.
155	21. `ResolveRoute` and `Execute` call `ensureFreshEnough(client_inputs,
156	    snapshot)` before scoring. The refresh set is derived from routing-relevant
157	    facts for the request: health, quota, model availability/discovery,
158	    context/tool/reasoning support, billing and effective-cost metadata when
159	    dynamic, and utilization when available. Autorouting may wait for these
160	    checks; correctness is more important than route latency.
161	22. A DDx server or other long-running client may maintain freshness by calling
162	    Fizeau's refresh/warmup entrypoints on a heartbeat. This is an optimization
163	    over the same synchronous, lock-coordinated contract. If no maintainer is
164	    running and `fiz models` observes stale fields, it should expose the stale
165	    status and suggest `fiz models --refresh` or starting a DDx freshness
166	    maintainer.
167	
168	### Eligibility and Pins
169	
170	23. Hard pins narrow the candidate set before scoring:
171	    - `Harness` means only that harness may be used.
172	    - `Provider` means only that provider source or selected endpoint may be
173	      used.
174	    - `Model` means only that exact model identity may be used.
175	24. Unpinned automatic routing excludes pay-per-token candidates unless the
176	    provider is included by default and metered routing is explicitly opted in
177	    by user config.
178	25. Pins override provider `include_by_default` and metered opt-in: a
179	    deliberately pinned default-deny pay-per-token provider can be considered.
180	26. Pins do not override policy `require[]`; `air-gapped` plus a remote
181	    provider pin fails.
182	27. Missing-power, inactive, deprecated, and exact-pin-only models are excluded
183	    from unpinned automatic routing. Exact model pins may still use them when
184	    the selected harness/provider can serve the model.
185	28. Hard gates are limited to explicit user constraints and dispatchability:
186	    pins, `require[]`, `no_remote`, metered opt-in, exact-pin support, and
187	    whether the candidate can actually be dispatched. Known-down endpoints,
188	    exhausted quota pools, and missing required context/tools/reasoning support
189	    are dispatchability failures. Cost, quality, non-fatal health risk, latency,
190	    utilization, and power fit are scoring inputs, not broad vetoes.
191	
192	### Power Scoring
193	
194	29. `MinPower` and `MaxPower` are soft scoring hints, not closed candidate
195	    lists, once a model has passed auto-routable eligibility.
196	30. A candidate below `MinPower` receives a stronger penalty than a candidate
197	    above `MaxPower`. This asymmetric scoring reflects failure risk: too weak
198	    is more likely to fail the task, while too strong is primarily a cost and
199	    latency concern.
200	31. If no power hints are supplied, model power contributes positively to the
201	    score alongside policy cost/placement preferences.
202	32. Exact `Model` pins keep exact identity. Policy-derived power bounds are
203	    still reported as evidence, but they do not substitute a different model.
204	
205	### Ranking
206	
207	33. Ranking considers:
208	    - policy baseline (`cheap`, `default`, `smart`, `air-gapped`);
209	    - catalog power;
210	    - provider billing and effective cost;
211	    - subscription shadow cost using PAYG-equivalent effective cost while
212	      retaining `actual_cash_spend=false`;
213	    - subscription quota health and burn-rate prediction;
214	    - route-health cooldown and observed reliability;
215	    - context headroom and required capabilities;
216	    - observed latency/speed;
217	    - endpoint utilization and saturation;
218	    - sticky affinity.
219	34. A qualified candidate is one that passes hard constraints, policy
220	    requirements, default-inclusion and metered opt-in gates, auto-routability,
221	    liveness, quota, and dispatchability. Power hints shape ranking inside that
222	    qualified set rather than replacing exact pins.
223	35. For a given policy and qualified set, Fizeau prefers the lowest effective
224	    cost candidate whose power fit is sufficient for the policy intent.
225	    A zero-cost but substantially underpowered candidate should not beat an
226	    in-band candidate for routine `default` work solely because it is free. A
227	    subscription model may have `actual_cash_spend=false` and still carry a
228	    PAYG-equivalent effective cost for scoring.
229	36. `cheap` selects the lowest effective-cost candidate with enough expected
230	    capability for the request. It should naturally prefer nano, mini, local, or
231	    fixed-cost candidates over maximum-quality frontier models when those
232	    cheaper candidates are available and sufficient; this is a scoring outcome,
233	    not a frontier-model exclusion gate.
234	37. `default` selects a routine balanced candidate. It should avoid maximum
235	    frontier models when lower-cost candidates satisfy the same routing
236	    constraints and expected capability.
237	38. Local/fixed candidates are preferred by `cheap` and `default` when they are
238	    eligible and capable. This preference never beats hard pins or
239	    `require[]`.
240	39. `smart` prefers higher-capability subscription/cloud routes when healthy
241	    and allowed.
242	40. `air-gapped` is local-only through `require=["no_remote"]`.
243	41. If at least one candidate is dispatchable under the user's explicit
244	    constraints, automatic routing must select one candidate even when the
245	    result is imperfect. If user constraints remove all candidates, routing
246	    fails clearly and attributes the failure to those constraints.
247	42. The router dispatches one selected candidate per request. Semantic retry or
248	    escalation belongs to the caller.
249	
250	### Status and Evidence
251	
252	43. `ResolveRoute` returns the selected candidate plus the full candidate
253	    trace, power policy, sticky evidence, utilization evidence, and the selected
254	    model's catalog-projected power.
255	44. `RouteStatus` reports recent decisions, cooldowns, provider reliability,
256	    sticky assignments, and routing-quality metrics. Routing quality is
257	    distinct from provider reliability.
258	45. Session logs and final events record the actual attempted route and failure
259	    class. They use v0.11 `policy` / `power_policy` fields.
260	46. When a route succeeds, fails, or rejects candidates, the evidence must be
261	    explainable from the same assembled snapshot facts exposed by `fiz models`
262	    plus request-local constraints.
263	47. Route evidence records the snapshot version, per-field freshness,
264	    refresh-failure status, effective cost, cost source, billing kind, and
265	    `actual_cash_spend` for selected and rejected candidates.
266	
267	## Acceptance Criteria
268	
269	| ID | Criterion | Suggested Verification |
270	|----|-----------|------------------------|
271	| AC-FEAT-004-01 | The embedded manifest is schema v5 and validates models, policies, providers, billing, and supported requirements. | `go test ./internal/modelcatalog ./...` |
272	| AC-FEAT-004-02 | `ListPolicies` returns exactly `air-gapped`, `cheap`, `default`, and `smart` with power bounds, `allow_local`, `require[]`, and manifest metadata. | `go test ./... -run ListPolicies` |
273	| AC-FEAT-004-03 | `cheap`, `default`, `smart`, and `air-gapped` produce the documented local/subscription/remote behavior under representative inventories. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run Policy` |
274	| AC-FEAT-004-04 | Pay-per-token providers are skipped in unpinned automatic routing unless provider default inclusion and metered opt-in both allow them, while explicit pins can select them. | `go test ./... -run 'IncludeByDefault|Metered'` |
275	| AC-FEAT-004-05 | Pins override default inclusion and metered opt-in but not `require[]`; `air-gapped` plus a remote pin returns `ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied`. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run Policy` |
276	| AC-FEAT-004-06 | Soft power scoring penalizes undershooting `MinPower` more than overshooting `MaxPower` and does not replace an exact model pin. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run Power` |
277	| AC-FEAT-004-07 | Route decisions consume the assembled snapshot, expose typed candidate rejection reasons, score components, selected endpoint/server instance, sticky evidence, and utilization evidence. | `go test ./... -run 'ResolveRoute|RouteStatus|routing_decision|ModelSnapshot'` |
278	| AC-FEAT-004-08 | `ResolveRoute` and `Execute` refresh stale routing-relevant snapshot fields through the lock-coordinated refresh path before scoring, while `fiz models` stays quick by default and `--refresh`/`--refresh-all` block explicitly. | `go test ./internal/discoverycache ./internal/modelregistry ./agentcli ./... -run 'Refresh|Fresh|Models'` |
279	| AC-FEAT-004-09 | Subscription candidates use PAYG-equivalent effective cost for scoring while surfacing `actual_cash_spend=false`; pay-per-token candidates are hard-gated only when dispatch would create actual metered spend without opt-in. | `go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'EffectiveCost|ActualCashSpend|Metered'` |
280	| AC-FEAT-004-10 | Removed v0.10 names are not advertised by policy listing, CLI help, or public service fields. | `go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz ./...` |
281	
282	## Constraints and Assumptions
283	
284	- Fizeau owns routing inside the embedded runtime; callers own semantic retry
285	  and cross-harness orchestration strategy.
286	- Provider configs remain transport/auth definitions.
287	- Catalog data can be refreshed explicitly, but normal request execution is
288	  offline with respect to manifest fetching. Runtime snapshot facts may refresh
289	  synchronously before autorouting when stale.
290	- Benchmark inputs inform power, but deployment class and cost prevent local
291	  community copies from tying managed frontier models solely on one benchmark.
292	
293	## Dependencies
294	
295	- `FEAT-003` for provider identity, billing, and default inclusion.
296	- `FEAT-005` for cost/session projections.
297	- `FEAT-006` for the CLI surface.
298	- `ADR-009` for the v0.11 naming and migration decision.
299	- `ADR-012` for assembled snapshot cache and harness-as-provider identity.
300	
301	## Out of Scope
302	
303	- User-authored route tables or per-request fallback chains.
304	- Automatic learning from routing-quality metrics.
305	- Network manifest refresh during ordinary execution.
306

1	package routing
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"sort"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/modelcatalog"
10	)
11	
12	// Request is the routing input. All fields are optional except at least
13	// one of {Policy, Model, Harness, Provider} should be set (otherwise the
14	// engine has nothing to disambiguate on).
15	//
16	// Provider is present from day one (fixes ddx-8610020e — no soft-preference
17	// dropping).
18	type Request struct {
19		Policy             string // "cheap" | "default" | "smart" | "air-gapped"
20		Model              string // exact concrete model pin
21		Provider           string // exact provider pin; constrains routing to one provider identity
22		Harness            string // hard preference; constrains routing to one harness
23		Reasoning          string // public reasoning scalar
24		Permissions        string // "safe" | "supervised" | "unrestricted"
25		ProviderPreference string // "local-first" | "subscription-first" | "local-only" | "subscription-only"
26		CorrelationID      string // validated sticky route key, when available
27		AllowLocal         bool
28		Require            []string
29	
30		// EstimatedPromptTokens, when > 0, drives context-window gating.
31		EstimatedPromptTokens int
32	
33		// RequiresTools, when true, requires the candidate to support tool calling.
34		RequiresTools bool
35		MinPower      int
36		MaxPower      int
37	
38		// ExcludedRoutes lists caller-supplied (Provider, Model, Endpoint)
39		// combinations to skip during candidate selection. The engine marks
40		// matching candidates ineligible with FilterReasonCallerExcluded.
41		ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute
42	}
43	
44	// ExcludedRoute is a caller-supplied exclusion hint for the routing engine.
45	// Provider is required; Model and Endpoint are optional (empty matches any).
46	type ExcludedRoute struct {
47		Provider string
48		Model    string // optional; empty matches any model on the provider
49		Endpoint string // optional; empty matches any endpoint
50	}
51	
52	const (
53		ProviderPreferenceLocalFirst        = "local-first"
54		ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionFirst = "subscription-first"
55		ProviderPreferenceLocalOnly         = "local-only"
56		ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionOnly  = "subscription-only"
57	
58		ContextSourceProviderAPI    = "provider_api"
59		ContextSourceProviderConfig = "provider_config"
60		ContextSourceCatalog        = "catalog"
61		ContextSourceDefault        = "default"
62		ContextSourceUnknown        = "unknown"
63	)
64	
65	// MinContextWindow returns the minimum context window the request requires,
66	// derived from EstimatedPromptTokens with a safety margin.
67	func (r Request) MinContextWindow() int {
68		if r.EstimatedPromptTokens <= 0 {
69			return 0
70		}
71		// 1.25x safety margin for response tokens + tool overhead.
72		return r.EstimatedPromptTokens + r.EstimatedPromptTokens/4
73	}
74	
75	const (
76		QuotaTrendUnknown    = "unknown"
77		QuotaTrendHealthy    = "healthy"
78		QuotaTrendBurning    = "burning"
79		QuotaTrendExhausting = "exhausting"
80	)
81	
82	// HarnessEntry is the harness-side input the caller (service) supplies.
83	// It is the routing engine's view of a registered harness; the engine does
84	// not import the harnesses package directly to keep the dependency narrow.
85	type HarnessEntry struct {
86		Name                string
87		Surface             string
88		CostClass           string
89		IsLocal             bool
90		IsSubscription      bool
91		IsHTTPProvider      bool
92		AutoRoutingEligible bool
93		TestOnly            bool
94		ExactPinSupport     bool
95		DefaultModel        string
96		SupportedModels     []string
97		SupportedReasoning  []string
98		MaxReasoningTokens  int
99		SupportedPerms      []string
100		SupportsTools       bool

1	package routehealth
2	
3	import (
4		"testing"
5	
6		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/provider/utilization"
7	)
8	
9	func TestUtilizationStoreEndpointLoadsUsesFreshSamplesAndLeaseFallback(t *testing.T) {
10		store := NewUtilizationStore()
11		store.Record("local", "desk-a", "model-a", utilization.EndpointUtilization{
12			ActiveRequests: utilization.Int(1),
13			QueuedRequests: utilization.Int(1),
14			MaxConcurrency: utilization.Int(4),
15			Source:         utilization.SourceVLLMMetrics,
16			Freshness:      utilization.FreshnessFresh,
17		})
18		store.Record("local", "desk-b", "model-a", utilization.EndpointUtilization{
19			ActiveRequests: utilization.Int(9),
20			QueuedRequests: utilization.Int(9),
21			MaxConcurrency: utilization.Int(10),
22			Source:         utilization.SourceLlamaMetrics,
23			Freshness:      utilization.FreshnessStale,
24		})
25	
26		loads := store.EndpointLoads("local", "model-a", map[string]int{
27			"desk-a": 2,
28			"desk-b": 1,
29		})
30	
31		if got := loads["desk-a"]; !got.UtilizationFresh {
32			t.Fatalf("desk-a fresh=%v, want true", got.UtilizationFresh)
33		} else if got.UtilizationSaturated {
34			t.Fatalf("desk-a saturated=%v, want false", got.UtilizationSaturated)
35		} else if got.NormalizedLoad != 2.5 {
36			t.Fatalf("desk-a normalized_load=%v, want 2.5", got.NormalizedLoad)
37		}
38	
39		if got := loads["desk-b"]; got.UtilizationFresh {
40			t.Fatalf("desk-b fresh=%v, want false for stale sample", got.UtilizationFresh)
41		} else if got.NormalizedLoad != 1 {
42			t.Fatalf("desk-b normalized_load=%v, want lease-count fallback 1", got.NormalizedLoad)
43		}
44	}
45	
46	func TestUtilizationStoreEndpointLoadsMarksSaturationFromFreshCapacity(t *testing.T) {
47		store := NewUtilizationStore()
48		store.Record("local", "desk-a", "model-a", utilization.EndpointUtilization{
49			ActiveRequests: utilization.Int(2),
50			QueuedRequests: utilization.Int(0),
51			MaxConcurrency: utilization.Int(2),
52			Source:         utilization.SourceVLLMMetrics,
53			Freshness:      utilization.FreshnessFresh,
54		})
55	
56		loads := store.EndpointLoads("local", "model-a", map[string]int{
57			"desk-a": 0,
58		})
59		got := loads["desk-a"]
60		if !got.UtilizationSaturated {
61			t.Fatalf("desk-a saturated=%v, want true", got.UtilizationSaturated)
62		}
63		if got.NormalizedLoad != 1 {
64			t.Fatalf("desk-a normalized_load=%v, want 1", got.NormalizedLoad)
65		}
66	}

1	package fizeau
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"io"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sync"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/harnesses"
12		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/routehealth"
13		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/serviceimpl"
14		sessionusage "github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/session"
15	)
16	
17	// FizeauService is the entire public Go surface of the fizeau module.
18	// See CONTRACT-003 for the full specification.
19	type FizeauService interface {
20		Execute(ctx context.Context, req ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
21		TailSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
22		ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error)
23		ListProviders(ctx context.Context) ([]ProviderInfo, error)
24		ListModels(ctx context.Context, filter ModelFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)
25		ListPolicies(ctx context.Context) ([]PolicyInfo, error)
26		HealthCheck(ctx context.Context, health HealthTarget) error
27		ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
28		RecordRouteAttempt(ctx context.Context, attempt RouteAttempt) error
29		RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
30	
31		// Historical session-log projections. CONTRACT-003 owns these so CLI
32		// subcommands such as log/replay/usage do not need to read the
33		// internal session-log JSONL schema.
34		UsageReport(ctx context.Context, opts UsageReportOptions) (*UsageReport, error)
35		ListSessionLogs(ctx context.Context) ([]SessionLogEntry, error)
36		WriteSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, w io.Writer) error
37		ReplaySession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, w io.Writer) error
38	}
39	
40	// ServiceConfig provides provider and routing data to the service without
41	// creating an import cycle from the root package into agent/config.
42	// Callers wrap their loaded *config.Config in a type that satisfies this interface.
43	type ServiceConfig interface {
44		// ProviderNames returns provider names in stable order (default first).
45		ProviderNames() []string
46		// DefaultProviderName returns the name of the configured default provider.
47		DefaultProviderName() string
48		// Provider returns the raw config values for a named provider.
49		Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
50		// HealthCooldown returns the configured cooldown duration (0 = use default 30s).
51		HealthCooldown() time.Duration
52		// WorkDir is the base directory for file-backed health state.
53		WorkDir() string
54		// SessionLogDir returns the configured sessions directory.
55		SessionLogDir() string
56	}
57	
58	// ServiceProviderEntry carries the minimal provider data the service needs.
59	type ServiceProviderEntry struct {
60		Type           string // "openai" | "openrouter" | "lmstudio" | "llama-server" | "ds4" | "omlx" | "lucebox" | "vllm" | "rapid-mlx" | "ollama" | "anthropic"
61		BaseURL        string
62		ServerInstance string
63		Endpoints      []ServiceProviderEndpoint
64		APIKey         string
65		Headers        map[string]string
66		Model          string // configured default model (may be empty)
67		Billing        BillingModel
68		// IncludeByDefault reports whether this provider participates in automatic
69		// routing when the caller does not pin a provider.
70		IncludeByDefault    bool
71		IncludeByDefaultSet bool
72		// ContextWindow is the configured provider-side context override.
73		ContextWindow int
74		// ConfigError marks this provider entry invalid while allowing the rest of
75		// the service config to load. Invalid providers are reported in status
76		// surfaces and excluded from routing.
77		ConfigError string
78		// DailyTokenBudget is the operator-configured per-UTC-day token budget
79		// (request + response) for this provider. Zero means no local burn-rate
80		// prediction; the provider's own quota signal still applies.
81		DailyTokenBudget int
82	}
83	
84	// ServiceProviderEndpoint is one configured provider serving endpoint.
85	type ServiceProviderEndpoint struct {
86		Name           string
87		BaseURL        string
88		ServerInstance string
89	}
90	
91	// ServiceOptions configures a FizeauService instance.
92	//
93	// seamOptions is embedded so production builds (no testseam tag) get an
94	// empty struct — making it a compile-time error to set seam fields without
95	// the build tag. Test builds inherit the four CONTRACT-003 seams
96	// (FakeProvider, PromptAssertionHook, CompactionAssertionHook,
97	// ToolWiringHook) automatically.
98	type ServiceOptions struct {
99		seamOptions
100	
101		ConfigPath string    // optional override; default $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fizeau/config.yaml
102		Logger     io.Writer // optional; agent writes structured session logs internally regardless
103	
104		// ServiceConfig, when non-nil, supplies provider and routing data for
105		// ListProviders and HealthCheck. Pass a value wrapping the loaded config.
106		// When nil, those methods return an error.
107		ServiceConfig ServiceConfig
108	
109		// QuotaRefreshDebounce is the minimum interval between live quota probes for
110		// a primary subscription harness. Zero uses the service default.
111		QuotaRefreshDebounce time.Duration
112		// QuotaRefreshStartupWait bounds startup waiting when the durable quota
113		// cache is missing, stale, or incomplete. Zero uses the service default.
114		QuotaRefreshStartupWait time.Duration
115		// QuotaRefreshInterval enables periodic refresh for long-running server
116		// processes. Zero disables the timer; cache refresh still happens on startup
117		// and service activity.
118		QuotaRefreshInterval time.Duration
119		// QuotaRefreshContext optionally cancels the periodic server refresh worker.
120		// When nil, the worker uses context.Background().
121		QuotaRefreshContext context.Context
122	
123		// CatalogProbeTimeout bounds live /v1/models discovery during routing.
124		// Zero uses the service default of 2s.
125		CatalogProbeTimeout time.Duration
126		// CatalogReloadTimeout bounds stale-while-revalidate catalog reloads.
127		// Zero uses the service default of 30s.
128		CatalogReloadTimeout time.Duration
129	
130		// LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens is the operator-supplied electricity/operations
131		// estimate for local endpoint providers under the embedded agent harness.
132		// Zero means local endpoint cost is unknown.
133		LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens float64
134		// SubscriptionCostCurve optionally overrides the default subscription
135		// effective-cost curve used by routing.
136		SubscriptionCostCurve *SubscriptionCostCurve
137	
138		// SessionLogDir overrides the directory used by historical session-log
139		// projections (UsageReport, ListSessionLogs, WriteSessionLog,
140		// ReplaySession). Empty falls back to ServiceConfig.SessionLogDir().
141		// Per-Execute requests still set their own
142		// ServiceExecuteRequest.SessionLogDir.
143		SessionLogDir string
144	
145		// StaleHarnessReaperGrace is the minimum age before a startup reaper may
146		// terminate an owned subprocess record. Zero uses the default grace window.
147		StaleHarnessReaperGrace time.Duration
148	}
149	
150	// SubscriptionCostCurve tunes effective subscription cost by quota utilization.
151	// Thresholds are percentages used, and multipliers are applied to the
152	// equivalent pay-per-token catalog rate.
153	type SubscriptionCostCurve struct {
154		FreeUntilPercent   int
155		LowUntilPercent    int
156		MediumUntilPercent int
157		LowMultiplier      float64
158		MediumMultiplier   float64
159		HighMultiplier     float64
160	}
161	
162	// QuotaState is a live quota snapshot for a harness. Nil means not applicable.
163	type QuotaState struct {
164		Windows    []harnesses.QuotaWindow `json:"windows"`
165		CapturedAt time.Time               `json:"captured_at"`
166		Fresh      bool                    `json:"fresh"`
167		Source     string                  `json:"source,omitempty"`
168		Status     string                  `json:"status,omitempty"` // ok|stale|unavailable|unauthenticated|unknown
169		LastError  *StatusError            `json:"last_error,omitempty"`
170	}
171	
172	// StatusError describes the most recent normalized status error for a harness,
173	// provider, or endpoint.
174	type StatusError struct {
175		Type      string    // unavailable|unauthenticated|error
176		Detail    string    // human-readable detail, safe for diagnostics
177		Source    string    // config path, endpoint, cache path, or probe name
178		Timestamp time.Time // zero when the source did not include a timestamp
179	}
180	
181	// AccountStatus describes authentication/account state without exposing
182	// provider-specific native files to consumers.
183	type AccountStatus struct {
184		Authenticated   bool
185		Unauthenticated bool
186		Email           string
187		PlanType        string
188		OrgName         string
189		Source          string
190		CapturedAt      time.Time
191		Fresh           bool
192		Detail          string
193	}
194	
195	// UsageWindow describes normalized usage attribution over a time window.
196	// Empty token/cost totals mean the service has no historical usage source yet.
197	type UsageWindow struct {
198		Name                string
199		Source              string
200		CapturedAt          time.Time

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a thorough summary for the user:

## Comprehensive Fizeau Codebase Exploration

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the fizeau codebase. Here's my complete findings:

### 1. Directory Structure (Main Components)

**Root-level service files:**
- `service.go` - Main FizeauService interface definition and service factory
- `service_routing.go` - Routing resolution implementation
- `service_probe.go` - Quota recovery probing logic
- `service_*.go` - Modular service implementations (catalog, events, execute, etc.)

**Key internal packages:**
```
internal/
├── routehealth/          # Route health tracking (your focus area)
│   ├── store.go          # Failure record storage and metrics
│   ├── lease_store.go    # Sticky route lease management
│   ├── utilization_store.go # Endpoint utilization tracking
│   └── *_test.go         # Comprehensive test suites
├── routing/              # Routing engine core
│   ├── engine.go         # Main routing logic
│   ├── score.go          # Scoring algorithm
│   └── *_test.go         # 50+ test files
├── provider/             # Provider implementations
│   ├── openai/
│   │   └── reachability.go # Error classification
│   ├── anthropic/
│   ├── vllm/, llama-server/, etc.
├── modelcatalog/         # Model metadata and policies
├── modelsnapshot/        # Assembled routing snapshot
├── quota/                # Quota state management
└── ... (25+ more packages)
```

### 2. Core Files Contents

**`internal/routehealth/store.go`** (242 lines)
- `Attempt` struct: Records availability feedback with Harness, Provider, Model, Endpoint, Status, Duration
- `Record` struct: Active route-attempt failure tracking
- `Store` struct: Thread-safe failure and metrics storage
- Key methods:
  - `RecordAttempt()` - Records success/failure, clears matching failures on success
  - `ActiveAttempts()` - Returns unexpired failures, removes expired ones
  - `MetricSignals()` - Returns success-rate and latency maps by provider/model key
  - `NormalizeKey()`, `Succeeded()`, `KeysMatch()` - Helper functions
- Supports TTL-based expiration (DefaultCooldown = 30s)

**`internal/routehealth/lease_store.go`** (270 lines)
- `Lease` struct: Sticky endpoint assignment with AcquiredAt, RefreshedAt, ExpiresAt
- `LeaseKey` struct: StickyKey identification
- `LeaseStore` struct: Thread-safe in-process sticky route assignments
- Key methods:
  - `Acquire()` - Stores/refreshes lease, preserves AcquiredAt on same provider/endpoint/model
  - `Live()` - Returns current lease if not expired
  - `Invalidate()`, `InvalidateEndpoint()` - Removes leases with reason tracking
  - `LiveByScope()`, `LeaseCounts()` - Query methods for provider/model scope
  - `LastInvalidation()` - Returns most recent invalidation reason
- DefaultLeaseTTL = 5 minutes

**`internal/routehealth/utilization_store.go`** (221 lines)
- `UtilizationKey` struct: Provider/Endpoint/Model identification
- `EndpointLoad` struct: Normalized load with fresh/saturated flags
- `UtilizationStore` struct: Thread-safe utilization sample storage
- Key methods:
  - `Record()` - Stores latest utilization sample
  - `Sample()` - Gets utilization with provider-wide fallback
  - `EndpointLoads()` - Normalizes load from fresh utilization + lease counts
- Uses `utilization.EndpointUtilization` from `internal/provider/utilization`
- Combines metrics: ActiveRequests, QueuedRequests, MaxConcurrency, CacheUsage

**`service_probe.go`** (253 lines)
- `QuotaRecoveryProber` func type: Probes quota_exhausted providers
- `runQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop()` - Periodic probe loop for recovery
- `probeOpenAIModels()` - GET /v1/models discovery with error classification
- Error classification:
  - `openai.ReachabilityError` for 5xx/transport failures
  - `ErrDiscoveryUnsupported` for 404 endpoints
  - Network failure detection helpers: `isNetworkFailure()`, `isServerError()`
- Bounded exponential backoff for failed probes

**`internal/provider/openai/reachability.go`** (219 lines)
- `ErrEndpointUnreachable` sentinel error
- `ReachabilityError` struct: Endpoint, Operation, StatusCode, Cause
- `HTTPStatusError` struct: Typed client-level HTTP errors
- `ShouldFailover()` function: Determines failover eligibility
  - Returns true for 5xx, auth (401/403), model-not-found 404, 429
  - Returns false for pinned, caller deadline, 400, generic 404
- `ClassifyHTTPStatus()`, `ClassifyNetwork()` - Error classification

### 3. Service Architecture

**Main Service Interface** (`service.go`):
```go
type FizeauService interface {
    Execute(ctx context.Context, req ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
    ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
    RecordRouteAttempt(ctx context.Context, attempt RouteAttempt) error
    RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
    // ... other methods for policies, providers, models, harnesses
}

func New(opts ServiceOptions) (FizeauService, error)
```

**Service Internals** (via `service_routing.go`):
- Private `service` struct holds:
  - Route health stores (failures, leases, utilization)
  - Model catalog and registry
  - Provider registry and quota state
  - Discovery cache
  - Session log manager
- `ResolveRoute()` implementation:
  1. Validates power bounds, role, correlation ID
  2. Builds routing inputs and snapshot
  3. Resolves model constraints
  4. Delegates to `routing.Resolve()`
  5. Applies sticky route leases
  6. Annotates evidence and caches decision

### 4. Test Patterns

**Store Tests** (`internal/routehealth/store_test.go`):
```go
func TestStoreRecordAttemptSuccessClearsMatchingFailure(t *testing.T) {
    store := NewStore()
    now := time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
    // Record failure, verify active
    store.RecordAttempt(Attempt{...Status: "failed"...})
    // Record success, verify cleared
    store.RecordAttempt(Attempt{...Status: "success"...})
    // Assert via ActiveAttempts() and MetricSignals()
}
```

**Lease Store Tests** (`internal/routehealth/lease_store_test.go`):
- Tests acquire/refresh/expire lifecycle
- Tests invalidation with reason tracking
- Tests concurrent key coexistence
- Uses deterministic timestamps and structured assertions

**Utilization Store Tests** (`internal/routehealth/utilization_store_test.go`):
- Tests fresh vs stale sample handling
- Tests lease count fallback when utilization unavailable
- Tests saturation detection from max concurrency
- Creates samples with `utilization.EndpointUtilization` helpers

**General Patterns:**
1. Use `time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)` for deterministic timestamps
2. Test via public methods; verify state with query methods
3. Use table-driven tests where validating multiple scenarios
4. Thread-safety tests use `sync.WaitGroup` for concurrent operations
5. Error returns checked immediately with `t.Fatalf()`
6. Component composition via constructor + assertion on aggregated state

### 5. Routing Pipeline

**Request Flow** (from `FEAT-004-model-routing.md`):
1. `ResolveRoute()` receives `RouteRequest` with policy, power hints, pins, constraints
2. Builds `routing.Request` with:
   - Policy name (cheap|default|smart|air-gapped)
   - Model/Provider/Harness pins
   - Power bounds (soft hints, not hard gates)
   - Required capabilities, context window, tools
   - ExcludedRoutes (caller-supplied skips)
3. Engine scores candidates considering:
   - Policy baseline preferences
   - Catalog power (1-10 scale)
   - Effective cost (PAYG-equivalent for subscriptions)
   - Route health (success rate, latency)
   - Utilization and saturation
   - Sticky affinity
   - Context headroom
4. Returns `RouteDecision` with selected candidate and full trace

**Health Tracking Integration:**
- `RecordRouteAttempt()` feeds failures into `Store` via `RecordAttempt()`
- Route health failures affect scoring (reliability factor)
- Sticky leases (`LeaseStore`) prefer recent successful routes
- Utilization (`UtilizationStore`) influences load balancing

### 6. Key Implementation Details

**Thread Safety:**
- All stores use `sync.RWMutex` or `sync.Mutex`
- Store methods are safe for concurrent use
- No global mutable state; stores are injected into service

**Time Handling:**
- All timestamps normalized to UTC
- Zero timestamps converted to `time.Now()` for defaults
- TTL-based expiration on reads (lazy cleanup)

**Error Classification** (per `openai/reachability.go`):
- `ReachabilityError` → 5xx, dial failures, TLS, connection reset
- `HTTPStatusError` → 4xx client errors
- Both implement `errors.Unwrap()` for traversal
- `ShouldFailover()` makes routing decisions based on error type

**Cost Scoring:**
- Subscriptions use "PAYG-equivalent" effective cost for ranking
- `actual_cash_spend` field separate from effective cost
- `CostSource` tracks origin (catalog, config, derived)

### 7. Important Files Not Modified

- `/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md` - Full routing spec
- `/CHANGELOG.md` - v0.14.0 added ExcludedRoutes support
- Comprehensive test coverage: 513 `_test.go` files
- No hardcoded provider-specific logic in health stores

### 8. Related Packages to Understand

- `internal/routing/engine.go` - Main routing decision engine
- `internal/routing/score.go` - Scoring formula
- `internal/provider/utilization/` - Utilization sample types
- `internal/modelcatalog/` - Catalog metadata and policies
- `internal/harnesses/` - Harness registry and info
- `internal/quota/` - Quota state and burn-rate tracking

This architecture cleanly separates concerns: health tracking (routehealth), routing decisions (routing), provider operations (provider), and service facade (service_*.go).

413	// Valid CachePolicy values.
414	const (
415		CachePolicyDefault = "default"
416		CachePolicyOff     = "off"
417	)
418	
419	// ValidateCachePolicy returns nil when v is one of the accepted CachePolicy
420	// values ("", "default", "off") and a typed error otherwise. The empty
421	// string is treated as "default".
422	func ValidateCachePolicy(v string) error {
423		switch v {
424		case "", CachePolicyDefault, CachePolicyOff:
425			return nil
426		default:
427			return fmt.Errorf("invalid CachePolicy %q: want \"\", %q, or %q", v, CachePolicyDefault, CachePolicyOff)
428		}
429	}
430	
431	// ValidatePowerBounds returns nil when the optional numeric routing power
432	// bounds are unset or coherent. Zero means "unset"; positive values are on
433	// the catalog's 1-10 power scale.
434	func ValidatePowerBounds(minPower, maxPower int) error {
435		if minPower < 0 {
436			return fmt.Errorf("invalid MinPower %d: must be >= 0", minPower)
437		}
438		if maxPower < 0 {
439			return fmt.Errorf("invalid MaxPower %d: must be >= 0", maxPower)
440		}
441		if minPower > 0 && maxPower > 0 && maxPower < minPower {
442			return fmt.Errorf("invalid power bounds: MaxPower %d must be >= MinPower %d", maxPower, minPower)
443		}
444		return nil
445	}

534		ContextLength int

536		ContextSource string

538		SourceStatus string

541		AutoRoutable bool
542		// ExactPinOnly reports whether the candidate is only eligible when pinned
543		// explicitly.
544		ExactPinOnly bool

558		ModelDiscoveryFreshnessAt     time.Time

563		// Utilization carries the normalized load sample used by the router.

583		// Power is the catalog model power used by automatic routing. Zero means
584		// unknown or not contributing.

588		// payload carries a single component bundle.

592		// LatencyMS is the observed median latency for this candidate, in

595		// SpeedTPS is the observed output speed in tokens per second. Zero when
596		// no observations are available.

598		// Utilization is the normalized load signal used to bias ranking. Zero
599		// means unknown or not contributing.

601		// SuccessRate is the observed success rate (0–1) for this candidate.
602		// Negative means insufficient data; zero means unknown.

605		// by routing. They are zero values for non-subscription candidates.
606		QuotaOK          bool

608		QuotaTrend       string
609		// Capability is a coarse capability score derived from the candidate's

613		// minimum required prompt window. Zero means unknown or no spare room.
614		ContextHeadroom int
615		// StickyAffinity is the bonus applied when the candidate matches the
616		// request's sticky server-instance assignment. Zero means no match.
617		StickyAffinity float64

622		PowerHintFit            float64 `json:"power_hint_fit"`
623		LatencyWeight           float64 `json:"latency_weight"`
624		PlacementBonus          float64 `json:"placement_bonus"`
625		QuotaBonus              float64 `json:"quota_bonus"`
626		MarginalCostPenalty     float64 `json:"marginal_cost_penalty"`
627		AvailabilityPenalty     float64 `json:"availability_penalty"`
628		StaleSignalPenalty      float64 `json:"stale_signal_penalty"`
629	}

805		// behavior; "off" disables caching for this request. Any other value is
806		// rejected at the service boundary (see ValidateCachePolicy). Providers
807		// that do not implement caching ignore the field; this field is read by
808		// the Anthropic cache_control writer (bead C) and the cache-aware cost
809		// attribution path (bead D).
810		CachePolicy string
811	
812		// Three independent timeout knobs:
813		//   Timeout         — wall-clock cap on the entire request.
814		//   IdleTimeout     — streaming-quiet cap; per-stream gap.
815		//   ProviderTimeout — per-HTTP-request cap to the provider.
816		Timeout         time.Duration
817		IdleTimeout     time.Duration
818		ProviderTimeout time.Duration
819	
820		// Optional native-loop overrides used when Harness == "fiz". These let
821		// the CLI and other callers route fully-resolved execution settings through
822		// the service path instead of maintaining a divergent direct loop path.
823		MaxIterations           int
824		MaxTokens               int
825		ReasoningByteLimit      int
826		CompactionContextWindow int
827		CompactionReserveTokens int
828	
829		// CostCapUSD is the per-run cost cap in USD, mirrored to
830		// internal/core.Request.CostCapUSD. When > 0, the native loop halts
831		// before issuing the next llm.request once running known cost plus the
832		// projected next-turn cost would meet or exceed the cap; the resulting
833		// final event reports Status="budget_halted". Zero means no cap. Per
834		// FEAT-005 §28 / AC-FEAT-005-07, the cap requires turn cost to be known;
835		// unknown-cost runs proceed past the cap with a stderr warning. Honored
836		// only when Harness=="fiz" (the native loop) — subprocess harnesses
837		// manage cost externally.
838		CostCapUSD float64
839	
840		// Optional stall policy. When non-nil agent enforces and ends with
841		// Status="stalled" if any limit hits. Default policy applies when nil.
842		StallPolicy *StallPolicy
843	
844		// SessionLogDir overrides the default session-log directory for this
845		// request (e.g. an execute-bead per-bundle evidence directory).
846		SessionLogDir string
847	
848		// SelectedRoute is the configured model-route name the caller picked
849		// (e.g. "code-pool"). Recorded into the service-owned session log so
850		// post-hoc routing analytics can correlate logs to route keys without
851		// reconstructing attribution from the event stream.
852		SelectedRoute string
853	
854		// Metadata is bidirectional: echoed back in every Event AND stamped
855		// onto every line of the session log so external consumers correlate.
856		Metadata map[string]string
857	
858		// Role tags the kind of work this call performs (e.g. "implementer",
859		// "reviewer", "decomposer", "summarizer"). Observational: echoed into
860		// the routing_decision and final event Metadata, plus the session-log
861		// header. Per CONTRACT-003 it is NOT part of the selection precedence
862		// chain (Day 1) and does NOT affect routing. Empty means unset.
863		//
864		// Normalization: lowercased, alphanumeric + hyphen only, max 64 chars;
865		// invalid values are rejected pre-dispatch with RoleNormalizationError.
866		Role string
867		// CorrelationID links calls that share work context (e.g.
868		// "bead_123:attempt_4") so reviewer/implementer/retry attempts can be
869		// joined in logs and aggregations. Observational: echoed into
870		// routing_decision and final event Metadata, plus the session-log
871		// header. Per CONTRACT-003 it is NOT part of the selection precedence
872		// chain (Day 1) and does NOT affect routing. Empty means unset.
873		//
874		// Normalization: printable ASCII (no control chars, no whitespace
875		// except hyphen, colon, underscore), max 256 chars; invalid values
876		// are rejected pre-dispatch with CorrelationIDNormalizationError.
877		CorrelationID string
878	}

880	// StallPolicy bounds how long the agent will spin without making progress
881	// before terminating with Status="stalled". A nil policy resolves to the
882	// default in service_execute.go.
883	type StallPolicy struct {
884		MaxReadOnlyToolIterations int // 0 = disabled
885	}

887	// service is the concrete FizeauService implementation.
888	type service struct {
889		opts     ServiceOptions
890		registry *harnesses.Registry
891		// harnessInstances holds the registered Harness implementation for
892		// each known harness name. The refresh scheduler iterates over this
893		// map, type-asserting each instance to the optional CONTRACT-004
894		// sub-interfaces (QuotaHarness, AccountHarness). harnessByName is
895		// the consultation seam.
896		harnessInstances map[string]harnesses.Harness
897		refreshScheduler *refreshScheduler
898		hub              *sessionHub
899	
900		// lastDecisionMu guards lastDecisionCache.
901		lastDecisionMu sync.RWMutex
902		// lastDecisionCache maps route key → (decision, time). Populated by
903		// ResolveRoute; read by RouteStatus.
904		lastDecisionCache map[string]lastDecisionEntry
905	
906		routeHealth      *routehealth.Store
907		routeLeases      *routehealth.LeaseStore
908		routeUtilization *routehealth.UtilizationStore
909	
910		// catalog is the service-scope model-catalog cache. Populated lazily
911		// on first use by routing + chat paths; shared across requests so the
912		// same endpoint isn't probed per-dispatch during a drain. See
913		// service_catalog_cache.go.
914		catalog *catalogCache
915	
916		// routingQuality records routing-quality observations (ADR-006 §5).
917		// Populated by Execute on every request and read by RouteStatus and
918		// UsageReport.
919		routingQuality *routingQualityStore
920	
921		// providerQuota is the per-provider quota state machine. Routing reads
922		// the projected exhausted set on every Resolve so quota_exhausted
923		// providers are excluded from candidate selection.
924		providerQuota *ProviderQuotaStateStore
925	
926		// providerBurnRate observes per-provider token consumption against the
927		// operator-configured daily_token_budget and pre-emptively transitions
928		// providers into quota_exhausted when local burn-rate predicts the
929		// budget will be exceeded before the next UTC daily reset.
930		providerBurnRate *ProviderBurnRateTracker
931	
932		runtime serviceimpl.Runtime
933	}

964	// loadServiceConfig, when non-nil, is called by New to load a ServiceConfig
965	// from a directory path when opts.ServiceConfig is nil. It is registered by
966	// the config package via init() to break the import cycle (config imports root).
967	var loadServiceConfig func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)
968	
969	// RegisterConfigLoader is called by the config package's init() to install the
970	// config-loading function. Do not call this from application code.
971	func RegisterConfigLoader(fn func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)) {
972		loadServiceConfig = fn
973	}
974	
975	// New constructs a FizeauService. When opts.ServiceConfig is nil, New attempts to
976	// load configuration automatically:
977	//  1. If opts.ConfigPath is non-empty, load from filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath).
978	//  2. Otherwise, load from the default global config location.
979	//
980	// Automatic loading requires the config package to be imported somewhere in the
981	// binary (it registers the loader via init). If the config package is not
982	// imported and ServiceConfig is nil, the service starts without provider config
983	// (ListProviders/HealthCheck will return errors until config is injected).
984	func New(opts ServiceOptions) (FizeauService, error) {
985		if opts.ServiceConfig == nil && loadServiceConfig != nil && shouldAutoLoadServiceConfig(opts) {
986			dir := ""
987			if opts.ConfigPath != "" {
988				dir = filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath)
989			}
990			sc, err := loadServiceConfig(dir)
991			if err != nil {
992				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent.New: load config: %w", err)
993			}
994			opts.ServiceConfig = sc
995		}
996		svc := &service{
997			opts:             opts,
998			registry:         harnesses.NewRegistry(),
999			harnessInstances: defaultHarnessInstances(),
1000			hub:              newSessionHub(),
1001			runtime:          serviceimpl.NewRuntime(serviceimpl.RuntimeDeps{}),
1002			catalog:          newCatalogCache(catalogCacheOptions{AsyncRefreshTimeout: opts.catalogRefreshTimeout()}),
1003			routeHealth:      routehealth.NewStore(),
1004			routeLeases:      routehealth.NewLeaseStore(),
1005			routeUtilization: routehealth.NewUtilizationStore(),
1006			routingQuality:   newRoutingQualityStore(),
1007			providerQuota:    NewProviderQuotaStateStore(),
1008			providerBurnRate: NewProviderBurnRateTracker(),
1009		}
1010		// Hydrate per-provider daily_token_budget from ServiceConfig so the
1011		// burn-rate tracker can predict exhaustion before the upstream quota
1012		// signal arrives.
1013		if opts.ServiceConfig != nil {
1014			for _, name := range opts.ServiceConfig.ProviderNames() {
1015				entry, ok := opts.ServiceConfig.Provider(name)
1016				if !ok {
1017					continue
1018				}
1019				if entry.DailyTokenBudget > 0 {
1020					svc.providerBurnRate.SetBudget(name, entry.DailyTokenBudget)
1021				}
1022			}
1023		}
1024		svc.reapStaleHarnessSessions()
1025		svc.ensurePrimaryQuotaRefresh(context.Background(), quotaRefreshStartup)
1026		svc.startPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorker()
1027		svc.startQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop()
1028		svc.refreshScheduler = newRefreshScheduler(svc.harnessByName, svc.registry.Names(), nil)
1029		svc.refreshScheduler.Start(context.Background())
1030		return svc, nil
1031	}
1032	
1033	// harnessByName returns the registered Harness instance for name. Returns
1034	// nil when no instance is registered under that name. Consumers
1035	// type-assert the result to the CONTRACT-004 sub-interfaces
1036	// (QuotaHarness, AccountHarness, ModelDiscoveryHarness) independently;
1037	// failed assertions mean the harness has not implemented that capability
1038	// (yet) and the caller MUST tolerate the absence.
1039	func (s *service) harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness {
1040		if s == nil {
1041			return nil
1042		}
1043		return s.harnessInstances[name]
1044	}

1046	// harnessType returns "native" for HTTP/embedded harnesses, "subprocess" for CLI-invoked ones.
1047	func harnessType(cfg harnesses.HarnessConfig) string {
1048		if harnessRunsInProcessOrHTTP(cfg) {
1049			return "native"
1050		}
1051		return "subprocess"
1052	}

1054	// supportedPermissions extracts the permission levels from PermissionArgs keys,
1055	// returning them in canonical order.
1056	func supportedPermissions(cfg harnesses.HarnessConfig) []string {
1057		if len(cfg.PermissionArgs) == 0 {
1058			return nil
1059		}
1060		order := []string{"safe", "supervised", "unrestricted"}
1061		var out []string
1062		for _, level := range order {
1063			if _, ok := cfg.PermissionArgs[level]; ok {
1064				out = append(out, level)
1065			}
1066		}
1067		return out
1068	}
1069	
1070	func supportedReasoning(cfg harnesses.HarnessConfig) []string {
1071		return append([]string(nil), cfg.ReasoningLevels...)
1072	}

1074	// claudeQuotaState reads the registered claude harness's QuotaStatus and
1075	// projects it onto the public CONTRACT-003 QuotaState surface via the
1076	// service_projection helpers. Per CONTRACT-004, the harness owns the
1077	// State enum and routing semantics; this method is the public-surface
1078	// projection — it neither inspects per-harness snapshot fields nor
1079	// re-derives status from Windows.
1080	func (s *service) claudeQuotaState(ctx context.Context) *QuotaState {
1081		qh, ok := s.harnessByName("claude").(harnesses.QuotaHarness)
1082		if !ok {
1083			return nil
1084		}
1085		status, err := qh.QuotaStatus(ctx, s.now())
1086		if err != nil {
1087			return &QuotaState{
1088				Status: "error",
1089				LastError: &StatusError{
1090					Type:      "error",
1091					Detail:    err.Error(),
1092					Source:    "claude.QuotaStatus",
1093					Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
1094				},
1095			}
1096		}
1097		return projectQuotaStatus(status)
1098	}

1100	// claudeAccountStatus reads the registered claude harness's
1101	// AccountStatus and projects it through service_projection helpers.
1102	// When the underlying snapshot carries no auth evidence (cold cache or
1103	// empty snapshot), the projection returns a zero-valued AccountStatus;
1104	// callers preserved the legacy nil-for-absent contract by returning nil
1105	// in that case.
1106	func (s *service) claudeAccountStatus(ctx context.Context) *AccountStatus {
1107		ah, ok := s.harnessByName("claude").(harnesses.AccountHarness)
1108		if !ok {
1109			return nil
1110		}
1111		snapshot, err := ah.AccountStatus(ctx, s.now())
1112		if err != nil {
1113			return nil
1114		}
1115		if !snapshot.Authenticated && !snapshot.Unauthenticated && snapshot.Email == "" && snapshot.PlanType == "" && snapshot.Detail == "" {
1116			return nil
1117		}
1118		return projectAccountSnapshot(snapshot)
1119	}

1121	// codexQuotaState reads codex quota evidence through the CONTRACT-004
1122	// QuotaHarness interface and projects it onto the public QuotaState.
1123	func (s *service) codexQuotaState(ctx context.Context) *QuotaState {
1124		qh, ok := s.harnessByName("codex").(harnesses.QuotaHarness)
1125		if !ok {
1126			return unavailableQuotaState("codex quota cache", "codex quota harness not registered")
1127		}
1128		status, err := qh.QuotaStatus(ctx, time.Now())
1129		if err != nil {
1130			return unavailableQuotaState("codex quota cache", err.Error())
1131		}
1132		if status.State == harnesses.QuotaUnavailable {
1133			return unavailableQuotaState("codex quota cache", "codex quota cache unavailable")
1134		}
1135		windows := append([]harnesses.QuotaWindow(nil), status.Windows...)
1136		return &QuotaState{
1137			Windows:    windows,
1138			CapturedAt: status.CapturedAt,
1139			Fresh:      status.Fresh,
1140			Source:     status.Source,
1141			Status:     quotaStatus(status.Fresh, windows),
1142		}
1143	}

1228	func (s *service) harnessUsageWindow(provider, since string) *UsageWindow {
1229		logDir := s.serviceSessionLogDir()
1230		if logDir == "" {
1231			return nil
1232		}
1233		now := s.now()
1234		report, err := sessionusage.AggregateUsage(logDir, sessionusage.UsageOptions{Since: since, Now: now})
1235		if err != nil || report == nil {
1236			return nil
1237		}
1238		var total sessionusage.UsageRow
1239		for _, row := range report.Rows {
1240			if row.Provider != provider {
1241				continue
1242			}
1243			total.Sessions += row.Sessions
1244			total.SuccessSessions += row.SuccessSessions
1245			total.FailedSessions += row.FailedSessions
1246			total.InputTokens += row.InputTokens
1247			total.OutputTokens += row.OutputTokens
1248			total.TotalTokens += row.TotalTokens
1249			total.CacheReadTokens += row.CacheReadTokens
1250			total.CacheWriteTokens += row.CacheWriteTokens
1251			total.UnknownCostSessions += row.UnknownCostSessions
1252			if row.KnownCostUSD == nil || total.UnknownCostSessions > 0 {
1253				total.KnownCostUSD = nil
1254			} else {
1255				if total.KnownCostUSD == nil {
1256					total.KnownCostUSD = new(float64)
1257				}
1258				*total.KnownCostUSD += *row.KnownCostUSD
1259			}
1260		}
1261		if total.Sessions == 0 {
1262			return nil
1263		}
1264		window := UsageWindow{
1265			Name:                since,
1266			Source:              logDir,
1267			CapturedAt:          now,
1268			Fresh:               true,
1269			InputTokens:         total.InputTokens,
1270			OutputTokens:        total.OutputTokens,
1271			TotalTokens:         total.TotalTokens,
1272			CacheReadTokens:     total.CacheReadTokens,
1273			CacheWriteTokens:    total.CacheWriteTokens,
1274			KnownCostUSD:        total.KnownCostUSD,
1275			UnknownCostSessions: total.UnknownCostSessions,
1276		}
1277		if total.KnownCostUSD != nil {
1278			window.CostUSD = *total.KnownCostUSD
1279		}
1280		return &window
1281	}

1290		}
1291		configDir = s.opts.ServiceConfig.SessionLogDir()

1297			Source: source,

1299			LastError: &StatusError{
1300				Type:      "unavailable",

1308	// ListHarnesses returns metadata for every registered harness.
1309	func (s *service) ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error) {
1310		s.ensurePrimaryQuotaRefresh(ctx, quotaRefreshAsync)
1311		statuses := s.registry.Discover()
1312	
1313		// Index statuses by name for O(1) lookup.
1314		statusByName := make(map[string]harnesses.HarnessStatus, len(statuses))
1315		for _, st := range statuses {
1316			statusByName[st.Name] = st
1317		}
1318	
1319		// Emit in registry preference order.
1320		names := s.registry.Names()
1321		out := make([]HarnessInfo, 0, len(names))
1322	
1323		for _, name := range names {
1324			cfg, ok := s.registry.Get(name)
1325			if !ok {
1326				continue
1327			}
1328			st := statusByName[name]
1329	
1330			info := HarnessInfo{
1331				Name:                 name,
1332				Type:                 harnessType(cfg),
1333				Available:            st.Available,
1334				Path:                 st.Path,
1335				Error:                st.Error,
1336				Billing:              harnessPaymentKind(name, cfg),
1337				AutoRoutingEligible:  cfg.AutoRoutingEligible,
1338				TestOnly:             cfg.TestOnly,
1339				ExactPinSupport:      cfg.ExactPinSupport,
1340				DefaultModel:         cfg.DefaultModel,
1341				SupportedPermissions: supportedPermissions(cfg),
1342				SupportedReasoning:   supportedReasoning(cfg),
1343				CostClass:            cfg.CostClass,
1344				CapabilityMatrix:     harnessCapabilityMatrix(name, cfg),
1345			}
1346			if !st.Available {
1347				info.LastError = statusError(st.Error, "harness discovery", time.Now())
1348			}
1349	
1350			// Populate live Quota for harnesses that have durable quota caches.
1351			switch name {
1352			case "claude":
1353				info.Quota = s.claudeQuotaState(ctx)
1354				info.Account = s.claudeAccountStatus(ctx)
1355			case "codex":
1356				info.Quota = s.codexQuotaState(ctx)
1357				info.Account = s.codexAccountStatus(ctx)
1358				info.UsageWindows = s.codexUsageWindows()
1359			case "gemini":
1360				info.Quota = s.geminiQuotaState(ctx)
1361				info.Account = s.geminiAccountStatus(ctx)
1362				info.UsageWindows = s.geminiUsageWindows()
1363			}
1364	
1365			out = append(out, info)
1366		}
1367	
1368		return out, nil
1369	}

1	package routing

3	import (

12	// Request is the routing input. All fields are optional except at least
13	// one of {Policy, Model, Harness, Provider} should be set (otherwise the
14	// engine has nothing to disambiguate on).
15	//
16	// Provider is present from day one (fixes ddx-8610020e — no soft-preference
17	// dropping).
18	type Request struct {
19		Policy             string // "cheap" | "default" | "smart" | "air-gapped"
20		Model              string // exact concrete model pin
21		Provider           string // exact provider pin; constrains routing to one provider identity
22		Harness            string // hard preference; constrains routing to one harness
23		Reasoning          string // public reasoning scalar
24		Permissions        string // "safe" | "supervised" | "unrestricted"
25		ProviderPreference string // "local-first" | "subscription-first" | "local-only" | "subscription-only"
26		CorrelationID      string // validated sticky route key, when available
27		AllowLocal         bool
28		Require            []string
29	
30		// EstimatedPromptTokens, when > 0, drives context-window gating.
31		EstimatedPromptTokens int
32	
33		// RequiresTools, when true, requires the candidate to support tool calling.
34		RequiresTools bool
35		MinPower      int
36		MaxPower      int
37	
38		// ExcludedRoutes lists caller-supplied (Provider, Model, Endpoint)
39		// combinations to skip during candidate selection. The engine marks
40		// matching candidates ineligible with FilterReasonCallerExcluded.
41		ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute
42	}
43	
44	// ExcludedRoute is a caller-supplied exclusion hint for the routing engine.
45	// Provider is required; Model and Endpoint are optional (empty matches any).
46	type ExcludedRoute struct {
47		Provider string
48		Model    string // optional; empty matches any model on the provider
49		Endpoint string // optional; empty matches any endpoint
50	}
51	
52	const (
53		ProviderPreferenceLocalFirst        = "local-first"
54		ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionFirst = "subscription-first"
55		ProviderPreferenceLocalOnly         = "local-only"
56		ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionOnly  = "subscription-only"
57	
58		ContextSourceProviderAPI    = "provider_api"
59		ContextSourceProviderConfig = "provider_config"
60		ContextSourceCatalog        = "catalog"
61		ContextSourceDefault        = "default"
62		ContextSourceUnknown        = "unknown"
63	)
64	
65	// MinContextWindow returns the minimum context window the request requires,
66	// derived from EstimatedPromptTokens with a safety margin.
67	func (r Request) MinContextWindow() int {
68		if r.EstimatedPromptTokens <= 0 {
69			return 0
70		}
71		// 1.25x safety margin for response tokens + tool overhead.
72		return r.EstimatedPromptTokens + r.EstimatedPromptTokens/4
73	}
74	
75	const (
76		QuotaTrendUnknown    = "unknown"
77		QuotaTrendHealthy    = "healthy"
78		QuotaTrendBurning    = "burning"
79		QuotaTrendExhausting = "exhausting"
80	)
81	
82	// HarnessEntry is the harness-side input the caller (service) supplies.
83	// It is the routing engine's view of a registered harness; the engine does
84	// not import the harnesses package directly to keep the dependency narrow.
85	type HarnessEntry struct {
86		Name                string
87		Surface             string
88		CostClass           string
89		IsLocal             bool
90		IsSubscription      bool
91		IsHTTPProvider      bool
92		AutoRoutingEligible bool
93		TestOnly            bool
94		ExactPinSupport     bool
95		DefaultModel        string
96		SupportedModels     []string
97		SupportedReasoning  []string
98		MaxReasoningTokens  int
99		SupportedPerms      []string
100		SupportsTools       bool
101	
102		// Available reflects the harness's discovered availability.
103		Available bool
104	
105		// QuotaOK / QuotaPercentUsed reflect live quota state (when applicable).
106		// SubscriptionOK gates subscription harnesses at the eligibility level:
107		// when false, the candidate is ineligible regardless of score.
108		QuotaOK          bool
109		QuotaPercentUsed int
110		QuotaStale       bool
111		QuotaTrend       string // unknown|healthy|burning|exhausting
112		QuotaReason      string
113		SubscriptionOK   bool // false = hard gate; true = score-based demotion
114	
115		// InCooldown marks the entire harness as being in a failure cooldown.
116		// When true the harness is demoted in score (via candidateInternal.InCooldown)
117		// but not hard-rejected, so it can still win when all other harnesses are
118		// also unavailable.
119		InCooldown bool
120	
121		// Providers is the list of providers this harness can dispatch to.
122		// For subprocess harnesses (claude/codex) this is typically a single
123		// vendor entry. For the native "fiz" harness it is the configured
124		// list of HTTP providers.
125		Providers []ProviderEntry
126	}
127	
128	// ProviderEntry describes one provider available under a harness.
129	type ProviderEntry struct {
130		Name                 string

132		ServerInstance       string
133		EndpointName         string

145

147		// A zero value with CostSourceUnknown means the provider cost is unknown.
148		CostUSDPer1kTokens float64
149		// CostSource describes where CostUSDPer1kTokens came from: catalog,
150		// subscription, user-config, or unknown.
151		CostSource string

163		ExcludeFromDefaultRouting bool
164	}

178	// full ranked list (including rejected ones with rejection reasons).
179	type Decision struct {

189	// Candidate is one ranked routing option.
190	type Candidate struct {
191		Harness            string
192		Provider           string
193		Billing            modelcatalog.BillingModel
194		Endpoint           string
195		ServerInstance     string
196		Model              string
197		Score              float64

199		CostSource         string

204		ScoreComponents    map[string]float64
205		Eligible           bool

207	
208		// FilterReason is the typed disqualification category, set at the
209		// rejection site that decided why this candidate is ineligible.
210		// Empty for eligible candidates. Service-layer code maps this to the
211		// public FilterReason* string constants without parsing free-form
212		// Reason text.
213		FilterReason FilterReason
214	
215		// LatencyMS, SuccessRate, and CostClass expose the score-component
216		// inputs so callers can render per-axis explanations alongside the
217		// final Score. Zero / negative values mean unknown (see Inputs docs).
218		LatencyMS       float64
219		SuccessRate     float64
220		CostClass       string
221		SpeedTPS        float64
222		Utilization     float64
223		ContextHeadroom int
224	
225		QuotaOK          bool

227		QuotaTrend       string
228		StickyAffinity   float64
229	}
